CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench repair. Printer replacement recommended; printer data cable and processor communication path to be evaluated if communication failure remains after printer replacement.

title: Philips HeartStart MRx Defibrillator - Error: Incompatible Printer
description: Troubleshooting the Incompatible Printer error on a Philips HeartStart MRx when the installed printer hardware does not match the expected software configuration, affecting printing, readiness checks, and documentation confidence.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the HeartStart MRx from patient use if the printer error affects documentation, event recording, readiness checks, or confidence in device operation. Replace it with another ready defibrillator before troubleshooting if active patient care is involved.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the device on and confirm the exact displayed error is Incompatible Printer. Note when the error appears and whether the printer operates, fails to print, or prevents readiness checks.

steps.title: Confirm the Installed Printer Type
steps.instructions: Inspect the installed printer assembly and identify whether a 50 mm or 75 mm printer is installed. Compare the installed printer width to the device configuration or facility standard.

steps.title: Check for Recent Service or Parts Replacement
steps.instructions: Review whether the printer assembly was recently replaced, swapped, or installed after repair. Check service records or parts documentation if available.

steps.title: Verify Software Configuration
steps.instructions: Check the device configuration to confirm what printer type the software expects. Confirm that the configured printer type matches the installed printer hardware.

steps.title: Check External Printer-Related Conditions
steps.instructions: Inspect the printer door, paper roll, paper seating, printer opening, debris, jammed paper, liquid intrusion, and impact damage to rule out separate external printer faults.

steps.title: Match the Hardware to the Expected Configuration
steps.instructions: If the software expects a 75 mm printer, verify that the correct 75 mm printer assembly is installed. Correct any hardware or configuration mismatch using approved service procedures.

steps.title: Retest the Device
steps.instructions: After confirming or correcting the printer hardware and configuration match, power cycle the device, run the operational check, and attempt a test print if allowed by facility procedure.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the correct printer assembly is installed and the error no longer appears, confirm final device status, printer function, test print result, and readiness check result before returning the unit to service.

CCR.Complaint: Philips HeartStart MRx displayed Incompatible Printer error during startup and printer function could not be confirmed.
CCR.Cause: Installed printer type did not match the printer type expected by the device software configuration.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service, verified printer type and configuration, and sent unit for correction of printer hardware/software mismatch before operational check and test print.

title: Philips HeartStart MRx Defibrillator - Error: SW Watchdog Fail
description: Troubleshooting SW Watchdog Fail errors on the Philips HeartStart MRx that may cause restart behavior, lockups, software instability, failed operational checks, or unreliable defibrillator operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the HeartStart MRx from patient use if SW Watchdog Fail appears during operation, startup, charging, monitoring, pacing, defibrillation readiness checks, or operational testing. Provide a known-good backup defibrillator before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on and confirm the exact displayed error is SW Watchdog Fail. Note whether it appears during startup, self-test, monitoring, charging, therapy mode selection, normal operation, or after restart.

steps.title: Translate the Error Into the Real-World Failure
steps.instructions: Treat the complaint as a software stability or system control issue. Ask whether the unit restarted unexpectedly, froze, became unresponsive, failed startup, or failed an operational check.

steps.title: Restart the Unit
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait briefly, and power it back on. Allow startup and self-check behavior to complete. If the error returns, treat it as recurring.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Verify known-good AC power, normal AC indicator behavior, proper battery seating, and adequate battery charge. Test with a known-good compatible battery if available.

steps.title: Inspect External Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the housing, display, controls, battery bay, AC input area, external ports, and therapy selector for damage, contamination, loose connectors, fluid intrusion, or evidence of impact.

steps.title: Remove Nonessential Accessories and Recheck
steps.instructions: Disconnect nonessential accessories such as ECG cable, SpO2 sensor, NBP hose, CO2 accessories, therapy cable, printer-related accessories, data card, or communication accessories. Restart and check whether the error returns.

steps.title: Run Available Operational Checks
steps.instructions: If the unit powers on successfully, perform the facility-approved operational check. Verify power, display, controls, therapy selector, battery indicators, AC indicators, accessory recognition if applicable, and absence of SW Watchdog Fail.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Error Is Recurring
steps.instructions: Classify the error as recurring if it returns after restart, appears during multiple power cycles, occurs during operational check, appears intermittently after clearing, or staff report repeated restarts or lockups.

steps.title: Reload Software if Approved and Supported
steps.instructions: If the error recurs, follow the approved Philips HeartStart MRx software support process used by the facility. Only trained and authorized personnel should perform software reloads using approved tools and version control.

steps.title: Verify After Software Reload
steps.instructions: After approved software reload, restart the device and complete required operational checks. Confirm the error does not return, startup is normal, controls respond, monitoring and therapy readiness pass, and configuration is correct.

CCR.Complaint: Philips HeartStart MRx reported SW Watchdog Fail error during startup. Staff removed the unit from service due to concern for unreliable defibrillator operation.
CCR.Cause: SW Watchdog Fail confirmed during troubleshooting. AC power, battery seating, visible damage, and accessory-related causes were checked. Error returned after restart, indicating recurring software watchdog failure requiring approved software reload or Philips escalation.
CCR.Resolution: Restarted unit, checked AC power and battery seating, inspected external condition, and removed nonessential accessories. Error recurred after restart. Unit labeled Out of Service and escalated for approved software reload or Philips Response Center support.

title: Philips HeartStart MRx Defibrillator - Error: Replace Clock Battery
description: Troubleshooting the Replace Clock Battery error on the Philips HeartStart MRx when date/time settings may not be retained and event documentation accuracy may be affected.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the defibrillator from patient care if the error is active or if date/time accuracy cannot be trusted. Provide clinical staff with a ready backup defibrillator before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the HeartStart MRx and confirm the exact Replace Clock Battery message. Check when the message appears and whether the device loses date/time settings.

steps.title: Check Main Power and Main Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Verify the main battery is installed, charged, and accepted by the device. Connect AC power if available and confirm external power is recognized.

steps.title: Confirm Date and Time Settings
steps.instructions: Check whether the date and time are incorrect, reset, or blank. Correct the date/time if needed, power cycle the device, and verify whether the settings are retained.

steps.title: Inspect for Obvious External or Handling Issues
steps.instructions: Check for physical damage, fluid intrusion, cracked housing, drop evidence, battery bay contamination, or external power connection damage.

steps.title: Determine Whether Service Is Required
steps.instructions: If the device powers normally but displays Replace Clock Battery, treat the issue as a service-level internal clock backup battery condition and remove the device from clinical use.

steps.title: Verify Correction After Approved Service
steps.instructions: After qualified service is completed, set the correct date/time and verify that the Replace Clock Battery message clears and date/time is retained after power cycling.

steps.title: Run Required Operational Checks
steps.instructions: Perform required post-service checks. Verify normal startup, correct date/time retention, acceptable battery status, recognized AC power, no additional errors, and passing operational checks.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the clock battery condition has been corrected, date/time is retained, and required checks pass, document the work and return the device to service.

CCR.Complaint: Philips HeartStart MRx displayed Replace Clock Battery error. Staff reported date/time issue after device startup.
CCR.Cause: Replace Clock Battery error confirmed. Date/time retention issue observed after power cycle. Main battery and AC power were checked with no external power issue found.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service. Clock backup battery condition corrected through approved service process. Reset date/time, verified time retention after power cycle, completed required operational checks, and returned unit to service.
